object_kind_model.definition: Durable domain truth with its own lifecycle and mutation rules.
Append-only or immutable record of a fact, attempt, decision, or observation.
Durable operational case with domain-local workflow and blockers.
Durable conversational or message-centric workflow lineage.
Derived read model or truth bridge for a surface or downstream consumer.
Condensed, read-optimized derivative for prioritization or concise display.
Published or compiled rule contract consumed across runtime boundaries.
Rule or policy source governing decision, visibility, or publication behavior.
Versioned bundle or package of related contracts, artifacts, or proofs.
Published manifest enumerating members, artifacts, or runtime composition.
Explicit tuple object used to bind scope, lineage, or publication facts.
Short-lived continuation or correlation token.
Capability or access grant governing what the holder may do.
Fenced ownership or exclusivity primitive with expiry and takeover semantics.
Gate or fence that must be satisfied before work may proceed or settle.
Explicit blocker object keeping work or closure open until satisfied.
Proof-bearing witness or certificate object.
Authoritative settlement or completion proof for an action or transition.
Current checkpoint that governs actionability, TTL, or admissibility.
Canonical event namespace member, milestone, signal, or envelope contract.
Descriptor or binding object that names runtime, surface, or scope shape.
Published or rendered artifact with governed presentation or proof role.
Namespace object used to group event or telemetry contracts.
Bounded concept retained because the corpus relies on it, but it does not fit a stronger primary kind.

families.label: Projection, digest, artifact, and surface descriptors
Policy, contract, bundle, manifest, tuple, namespace, and event contracts
